2605.25045.title: AION: Next-Generation Tasks and Practical Harness for Time Series
2605.25045.paper_summary: Time series research is moving beyond fixed forecasting benchmarks toward realistic tasks that combine prediction, contextual reasoning, tool use, and structured decision support. Most benchmarks are built around clean data and short evaluation loops; agents alone may miss temporal constraints, evidence checks, or review before finalizing outputs. We first formalize next-generation time series tasks as three-component tuples consisting of a task file, a workspace, and a validation interface. We then present AION, a time series harness built from six component groups: agents, skills, rules, memory, evaluation, and protocols. In this harness, we use three design principles: temporal grounding, temporal knowledge-grounded reasoning, and reliability mechanisms such as post-experiment analysis and layered review. A Kaggle Store Sales case study shows that the harness produces more detailed process traces, more artifacts, and more review steps than the same base agent operating in OpenCode direct build mode. Taken together, these results argue for a paradigm shift from fixed tasks to realistic ones under real-world constraints.
2605.25045.paper_summary_zh: 時間序列研究正從固定的預測基準轉向結合預測、情境推理、工具使用和結構化決策支持的現實任務。大多數基準是基於乾淨的數據和短期評估循環；僅依賴代理可能會忽略時間限制、證據檢查或在最終確定輸出之前的審查。我們首先將下一代時間序列任務形式化為由任務文件、工作空間和驗證介面組成的三元組。然後，我們介紹AION，一個由六個組件組成的時間序列工具：代理、技能、規則、記憶、評估和協議。在這個工具中，我們使用三個設計原則：時間基礎、時間知識基礎推理，以及可靠性機制，如實驗後分析和分層審查。一個Kaggle商店銷售的案例研究顯示，這個工具產生了比在OpenCode直接構建模式下運行的相同基礎代理更詳細的過程痕跡、更多的工件和更多的審查步驟。綜合這些結果，這些結果主張從固定任務轉向在現實世界約束下的現實任務的範式轉變。
2605.25045.author: Tianxiang Zhan et.al.
2605.25045.authors: Tianxiang Zhan, Xiaobao Song, Tong Guan, Shirui Pan, Ming Jin
